questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. Henry is so <u>servile</u> that other people take advantage of him.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. Her dog can climb <u>under</u> the fence.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word ‘Optimistic’ from the given sentence. After the incident, Radha prepared herself to stay positive even in gloomy days.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. He was <u>compelled</u> by everyone to join the group.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word given in brackets to fill in the blank. Mr. Khanna is quite ­­___________(callous) towards the victims of earthquake.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. Do you know the old <u>adage</u> 'the show must go on’?
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. The thief was caught with a <u>spurious</u> Picasso painting.

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. His house was <u>filthy</u> with papers and eatables strewn everywhere.

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. Those streets are very <u>lonely</u>.

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. To <u>abate</u> her listlessness, she went for a long walk in the country.

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. The professor's lectures are very conventional and so <u>tedious</u>.
